tagline: eight fixes to patroni's shutdown sequence, exception handling, and thread pool lifecycle
editionNote: Eight merged PRs across patroni this week — mostly lifecycle and compatibility work, no releases.

articles.headline: the backoff patroni needed to stop over-eager failovers
articles.deck: *A 200ms network hiccup no longer demotes your primary.*
articles.body: Patroni used to pull the failover trigger the moment a heartbeat gap appeared, even fleeting ones. @cyberdem0n's #3453 adds a primary race backoff — now a brief network stutter doesn't declare the leader dead. The delay sits in the leader race detection path, buying time before the cluster panics and promotes a replica.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a pendulum swinging, frozen mid-arc between two points

articles.headline: etcd v3's Unavailable exception now lands somewhere safe
articles.deck: *Patroni stops going blind when the store is temporarily unreachable.*
articles.body: The Unavailable exception from etcd v3 was propagating uncaught, leaving patroni without cluster state visibility during transient store outages. #3562 catches and handles it properly. Now patroni degrades gracefully when the consensus store hiccups instead of crashing blind.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a hand catching a falling object before it hits the ground

articles.headline: global thread pool now shuts down last
articles.deck: *The shutdown sequence was demoting threads while they were still needed.*
articles.body: #3569 reorders patroni's shutdown lifecycle — the global thread pool now terminates after everything else has finished. Before, other components tried to schedule work on a pool that was already dead, causing silent failures during shutdown.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a series of gears, one grinding to a halt while others still turn

articles.headline: patroni now handles threading changes in python 3.11+
articles.body: Python 3.11+ changed how the threading module works internally, breaking some of patroni's assumptions about thread lifecycle. #3526 adds compatibility logic to handle the new behavior. Patroni users on newer Python versions no longer encounter threading-related failures.
articles.illustrationPrompt: two different types of locks lying on a table side by side

articles.headline: postgresql.parameters type-checked on load
articles.deck: *Config errors surface immediately instead of at runtime.*
articles.body: #3568 adds validation that postgresql.parameters is a dictionary. Malformed configs that sneak past the parser now fail early with a clear error message instead of causing cryptic failures later.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a hand holding a structure, examining it from different angles

articles.headline: PostgreSQL config no longer reloads during custom bootstrap
articles.body: Patroni was reloading the PostgreSQL configuration while a custom bootstrap was mid-flight, interfering with the bootstrap process. #3563 defers config reload until after bootstrap completes. Custom initialization scripts now execute without interruption.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a hand pausing a clock mid-tick

articles.headline: CitusHandler.run() postponed until needed
articles.body: #3571 defers CitusHandler initialization until it's actually required. The change trims startup overhead for installations not using Citus.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a shelf of items waiting, untouched and still boxed

articles.headline: unit tests no longer block on time.sleep()
articles.body: #3570 removes direct time.sleep() calls from unit tests. Tests that were burning real wall-clock time now run instantly, making the test suite faster and less flaky.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a stopwatch face showing zero elapsed time

closingNote: @cyberdem0n put in a quiet week of maintenance — no releases, no drama, just the plumbing that keeps clusters from falling apart.
